patch.value:
pm eval measures SEARCH ranking quality (nDCG/MRR golden queries, shipped via pm-u8n5/pm-22x2) but nothing measures whether pm context / pm next assemble the RIGHT working set — the core promise of 'project management = context management'. The adaptive relevance engine (pm-4k6b) will change assembly behavior; without a quality harness we cannot prove it is an improvement, tune signal weights, or catch regressions.

Proposal: extend the eval pattern from search to context assembly.
- Golden workspaces: a small real-shaped snapshot plus pm-mi2x synthetic workspaces at 10k/100k scale.
- Graded judgments: given agent state (focus item, active claim, plan step, query), the ideal context set with per-item relevance grades.
- Rank-aware scoring (nDCG-style) over the assembled context and next ordering, plus token-budget adherence checks.
- Phase 1 (now): baseline today's structural assembler — same before/after role pm-a22j plays for token cost.
- Phase 2 (with pm-4k6b): per-signal attribution in eval output so weight tuning is evidence-driven; gate in CI like the search eval.

SDK-first: the runner consumes only public SDK read primitives (pm-rjqr), so custom scorers from extensions are evaluable with the same harness.
Context relevance evaluation harness: graded golden-workspace judgments for pm context/next quality

Golden corpus (one real-shaped and one synthetic pm-mi2x workspace) with graded judgments versioned in-repo; offline runner scores pm context and pm next with rank-aware quality metrics; baseline of the current structural assembler recorded before pm-4k6b lands; CI gate fails on quality regression beyond a documented threshold; harness consumes only public SDK read primitives; documented workflow for adding new judgment cases

author: codex-agent
message: Split context relevance evaluation harness into implementation slices

author: codex-agent

patch.value: Evaluation harness for context quality: graded workspaces and continuity scenarios that measure whether pm context/next return the right items under explicit token budgets.

message: Backfill concise description after splitting context evaluation into child slices

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Planning evidence 2026-07-07: pm-atfm remains the canonical quality harness for context/next relevance. Added non-duplicate child slices pm-lixy (graded scenario corpus across scratch, real-shaped, scale, and returning-agent continuity cases) and pm-xmp5 (offline runner + CI gate). Done definition should mirror search-eval rigor but score context assembly: rank-aware quality, required-item recall, continuity-delta coverage, and token-budget adherence. This keeps pm-4k6b weight tuning evidence-driven instead of aesthetic.

value.author: codex-root-20260709
value.text: Verification evidence 2026-07-10: full sandbox suite passed 270 files / 5,505 tests with exact 100% statements, branches, functions, and lines. pm test-all over the seven active items passed 8 linked commands with 0 failures and 1 duplicate-safe skip. pnpm typecheck, contracts:check, lint (ESLint + 0 jscpd clones + static quality/token budget), docs-skills, context-eval, security scan, and audit all passed. Live CodeFactor reports A+ and 0 issues. Fresh packed npm install at /tmp/pm-cli-sdk-path-proof-XIsSsh activated 11 bundled packages in a path-target tracker, left caller extension files at zero, and passed CLI/SDK context plus isolated doctor with zero blocking failures. pm health is clean and history drift is zero.
